violations.description: General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. Both handwash sinks blocked at the beginning of the inspection. Watched employee change tasks and gloves without washing hands.
Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. Open box of fish on the floor up front.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Food removed from original packaging was not placed in a clean and sanitized container. Bare food items in Walmart bags. Need to be food grade bags.--Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. Raw beef and chicken above vegetables in the refrigerator. Raw fish above vegetables in the food prep cold hold.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --In-use utensil was not properly stored during pauses in food preparation or dispensing. Single serve scoop floating in sauce container with no handle.
Thawing potentially hazardous food. — --Potentially hazardous food was not thawed in an approved manner. Thawing raw fish in the front handwash sink.

Time as a public health control. — --Food was not marked or identified with the time within which it must be cooked, served, or discarded. Sushi rice up front is at 115 F and not time stamped. Needs to be time stamped and discarded every 4 hours.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. Contact paper on the top of the freezer. Bare wood under the front counter.
Storage of cleaned equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not covered or inverted during storage. Clean plates and bowls need to be inverted or covered.

Handling of clean equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not handled in a manner to prevent contamination of food-contact surface. Silverware in holders needs to be facing the same direction.

Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was not provided in sufficient number and/or not accessible to allow convenient use by employees. Both handwash sinks were blocked at the beginning of the inspection.--Handwashing lavatory was used for purpose other than handwashing. Thawing fish in the front handwash sink.
Toilet facilities. — --Toilet room used by women did not contain at least one covered waste receptacle.--Toilet room was not provided with sufficient mechanical ventilation.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Floors in the facility need cleaned. Raw fish juice was dripped on the floor.

Handling and storage of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Poisonous or toxic material was not stored in a manner to protect against contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items . Bottle of medicine on the shelf right above the food prep area.
